findings.message: Command frontmatter missing 'allowed-tools' field. All 25 spartan commands share this systemic gap.

Command frontmatter missing 'allowed-tools' field. Orchestration commands like gsd also need Task for sub-agent dispatch.
Command frontmatter missing 'allowed-tools' field. Also missing empty-input guard for required args[0].
Command uses '{{ args[0] }}' as required argument with no Jinja default. Invoking /spartan:guard with no argument raises TemplateError before the command body executes.

When permInteractive is false (the default), the bridge engine silently sets permissionMode='bypassPermissions' and allowDangerouslySkipPermissions=true with all tools allowed. Users adopting the bridge without reading the default get a maximally-permissive agent.
node-telegram-bot-api@^0.66.0 and dotenv@^16.4.7 use caret ranges — minor/patch updates install automatically, creating supply chain risk.
Agent has zero concrete input/output example blocks.
Agent has zero concrete input/output example blocks.
Body uses vague quantifier 'when helpful' without specifying concrete conditions.

Agent body instructs Claude to run 'cat .spartan/config.yaml', 'ls rules/', and 'ls .claude/rules/' but the tools frontmatter declares only ["Read","Grep","Glob","WebSearch"] — Bash is absent. These shell calls will fail at runtime.
Agent has zero concrete input/output example blocks. Examples are the primary calibration mechanism for agent behavior.

Branch name from git output is concatenated into execSync shell command at lines 174 and 181. Sanitization exists (line 169) but allows '/' which could permit git ref path traversal. Risk is locally bounded but pattern is unsafe.
js-yaml@^4.1.1 uses caret range — minor/patch updates install automatically.
Script sources .spartan/ai.env, .env, and ~/.spartan/ai.env for API keys. Expected behavior for a local development tool; no credentials logged or exfiltrated.
'npx get-shit-done-cc@latest' executes whatever the npm registry serves as latest at run time. A compromised publish could execute arbitrary code on the installer's machine.
Skill lacks a formal ## Gotchas section despite having comprehensive code templates.
Skill uses 'Common Mistakes' instead of the canonical ## Gotchas section name.
Skill lacks both a ## Gotchas section and formal examples. Has rules and checklists but missing failure-pattern documentation.
Skill has 'What to Avoid' content inline but lacks a formal ## Gotchas section.
Skill lacks a formal ## Gotchas section.
Skill lacks a formal ## Gotchas section.
The SKILL.md frontmatter is missing the required allowed_tools field as specified in SKILL_AUTHORING.md. All skills must declare allowed_tools for Claude Code to validate tool access at load time.
Skill lacks a formal ## Gotchas section.
Skill has extensive inline bad/good examples but lacks a formal ## Gotchas section.
Skill lacks a formal ## Gotchas section.
Skill lacks a formal ## Gotchas section as required by SKILL_AUTHORING.md. Missing 3+ documented failure patterns.
Skill has extensive rules but lacks a formal ## Gotchas section with failure patterns.

Agent body instructs Claude to run 'cat .spartan/config.yaml', 'ls rules/', and 'ls .claude/rules/' but the tools frontmatter declares only ["Read","Grep","Glob","WebSearch"] — Bash is absent. These shell calls will fail at runtime.
The SKILL.md frontmatter is missing the required allowed_tools field as specified in SKILL_AUTHORING.md. All skills must declare allowed_tools for Claude Code to validate tool access at load time.
